hypothesis_en: Mr President, paragraph 51 of this Parliament's recent resolution on the outcome of the European Council states that, without strong pressure on both sides, the roadmap will remain ineffective. In my opinion, that government has not only not assumed its responsibility, but has been handed over to the government of Israel. The proof of this is that the Israeli government's Minister without Portfolio, Gideon Ezra, has stated this Friday that Powell's comments aimed at softening Bush's pro-Israeli tone do not reflect Bush's own opinion. In my opinion, the Palestinian National Authority has been waiting for months for the resumption of these negotiations, but it is clear that Israel does not want to negotiate with the Palestinian Authority and is currently, with the full support of Bush, preparing for a unilateral withdrawal from Gaza. It is already clear enough in my opinion that this is not a Gaza first, followed by a West Bank second, but a Gaza only. I ask this Parliament, the Council and the Commission to consider the possibility of fully applying Article 2 of the Association Agreement with Israel in the event of the continuation of the policy of extrajudicial executions. Likewise, and finally, paragraph 49 calls on the Council to invite the Member States of the Union which are members of the Security Council to present immediately a proposal to establish an international peacekeeping force at the 1967 borders with the mandate to protect both the Israeli and Palestinian people from terrorist attacks and military incursions and interventions. Finally, we must also reiterate this demand.
reference_en: (5.78) item fifty-one in a recent resolution from the European Parliament on the results of the European Council states that without considerable pressure on both sides the road map will continue to be ineffective of course in my opinion the road map is still ineffective and I'm wondering where pressure is being exerted on Israel the European Parliament is also asking the American government to assume its responsibilities in the present crisis and in my opinion that government is not only failing to assume its responsibilities but also (1.68) is in the hands of the Israeli government the declarations made by Mr Powell last Thursday are a confusion of wishful thinking and cynicism when he says that the meeting between Bush and Sharon produced positive results for the Palestinians and their aspirations to a state proof of this is the fact that the Gideon Ezra a minister without portfolio in the Israeli government said that Powell's statements (1.11) which intended to tone down the pro-Israeli opinions of Bush did not actually reflect the opinions of Bush himself the resolution of this Parliament is also asking for both sides to make serious efforts to resume authentic (1.06) negotiations (1.21) in my view the Palestinian National Authority has been waiting for this for w= months it's been waiting for negotiations to be resumed but it's clear that Israel doesn't wish to negotiate with the Palestinian authority and at present with the full support of Bush it is preparing for unilateral withdrawal from Gaza outside the road map I think it's clear enough in my opinion that this is not Gaza first followed by West Bank second but it's Gaza only after all these events we should remember a couple of other points on our resolution forty-eight included after the assassination of Sheik Yassin calls upon (3.92) the Council and the Commission to look at the possibility of applying fully Article two in the Association Agreement with Israel if it continues its policy of extra judiciary executions end of quote well the extra judiciary executions are still continuing and so in my opinion we need to reiterate to the Council and the Commission the request from this Parliament and finally item forty-nine calls upon Council to ask the Member States of the Union which form part of the Security Council (1.11) to table a proposal immediately to establish an international peacekeeping force (1.05) on the seventy-seven borders with a view to protecting the people of Israel and Palestinian against terrorist attacks and military interventions and we should also reiterate that requirement thank you (1.36)
source_es: yes Presidente el punto cincuenta y uno de la reciente Resolución de este Parlamento sobre los resultados del Consejo Europeo afirma que textual sin una fuerte presión sobre ambas partes la hoja de ruta seguirá siendo ineficaz desde luego en mi opinión la hoja de ruta sigue siendo ineficaz y me pregunto dónde está la presión sobre Israel el Parlamento pide asimismo textual al Gobierno americano que asuma su responsabilidad en la crisis actual en mi opinión dicho Gobierno no solamente no ha asumido su responsabilidad sino que se ha entregado al Gobierno de Israel las declaraciones de Powell del pasado jueves son mezcla en mi opinión de un ridículo wishful thinking y un cierto cinismo cuando dice el Secretario que la reunión de Bush y Sharon ha producido resultados positivos para los palestinos y sus aspiraciones a un estado (1.06) prueba de ello es que el ministro sin cartera del Gobierno de Israel Gideon Ezra ha declarado este viernes que los comentarios de Powell destinados a suavizar el tono pro-israelí de Bush no reflejan la opinión del propio Bush (1.16) la resolución de este Parlamento pide también por otro lado que ambas partes textual hagan serios esfuerzos para reanudar unas auténticas conversaciones u= perdón negociaciones (1.21) en mi opinión la Autoridad Nacional Palestina las ha estado esperando durante meses la reanudación de esas negociaciones pero está claro que Israel no quiere negociar con la Autoridad Palestina y se dispone en estos momentos con el pleno apoyo de Bush a una retirada unilateral de Gaza fuera del marco de la hoja de ruta está ya suficientemente claro en mi opinión que no se trata de un Gaza first seguido de un West Bank second sino de un Gaza only después de todos estos acontecimientos conviene recordar otros dos puntos de nuestra resolución el punto cuarenta y ocho incorporado tras el asesinato del jeque Yassin dice pide al Congreso este Parlamento perdón piede este Parlamento al Consejo y a la Comisión que examinen la posibilidad de aplicar plenamente el artículo dos del Acuerdo de Asociación con Israel en caso de que continúe la política de ejecuciones extrajudiciales fin de la cita pues bien las ejecuciones extrajudiciales continúan la última con Rantisi debemos pues en mi opinión reiterar al Consejo y a la Comisión la petición de este Parlamento asimismo y finalmente el punto cuarenta y nueve pide al Consejo que invite a los Estados miembros de la Unión que ~aforman parte del Consejo de Seguridad a que presenten inmediatamente una propuesta para establecer una fuerza internacional de mantenimiento de la paz en las fronteras de mil novecientos sesenta y siete con el mandato de proteger tanto al pueblo israelí como al palestino de ataques terroristas y de incursiones e intervenciones militares fin de la cita debemos también reiterar esta exigencia muchas gracias
